description_long: This is a small bird with a streamlined body, featuring a dark head and upper back that transitions into lighter grayish-brown tones along the sides and underbelly. The wings are compact and closely held to the body, with subtle feather layering visible along the edges. Its beak is short and straight, well-suited for generalist feeding. The tail is relatively short and appears slightly rounded at the tip, with a subtle gradation in feather length. The legs are not clearly visible, but the overall posture suggests a perching bird commonly found in urban parks and gardens. The plumage is smooth with a gentle gradient from dark to light, lacking prominent spots or stripes.
description: A small, streamlined bird with a dark head, grayish-brown body, and a short, rounded tail. The plumage is smooth and subtly graded from dark to light.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's dark head, smooth transition to a lighter grayish-brown body, and the full length of its short, rounded tail.
description_view_1: Front angled view highlighting the rounded head, short beak, and the compact, slightly puffed chest.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the lighter underbelly and the subtle feather layering along the wing and tail.
description_view_3: Rear angled view focusing on the rounded tail and the back of the head, with the body tapering towards the tail.
attribute_plumage_pattern: smooth gradient from dark head to lighter grayish-brown body, no distinct spots or stripes
